title: Waywa Fybot

{{Quote|A gentle being, our Waywa; at heart he nurtures no ambition greater than to become a gourmet chef. I suspect that you and I would find his culinary efforts quite resistible.|Rokur Gepta, to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
'''Waywa Fybot''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as an undercover narcotics agent in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. A native of the Quor'sav [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Uaua/Legends|Uaua]], Fybot originally trained as a gourmet chef before being forcibly conscripted into Imperial service. Fybot ultimately entered the employ of [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]], under the guise of wealthy Oseon industrialist [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]], who promised Fybot freedom from the Empire in exchange for helping him entrap offworld [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. However, when Fybot delivered Calrissian to Gepta on [[Oseon 5792]], the Sorcerer rewarded Fybot only with a quick [[Death/Legends|death]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|The order for your arrest, sir, originated in the highest possible echelons. The very highest possible echelons. In addition, shortly before I was dispatched to this system, I was given purely verbal instructions, sir, that you were not intended to survive the process.|Waywa Fybot, to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] of the avian [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]],<ref name="cswe" /> Waywa Fybot grew up on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Uaua/Legends|Uaua]],<ref name="Gamer5" /> experiencing what he remembered as an idyllic upbringing in a small town. Fybot dreamed of becoming a gourmet chef in his younger years, and was studying at culinary school when he was forcibly conscripted into service under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Fybot was taken offworld and trained in [[Police officer/Legends|law enforcement]], but continued to send [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] to his parents on Uaua for years after his departure. Fybot found his new line of work as an undercover narcotics agent, operating in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality's]] [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] amidst some of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] wealthiest beings. It was in Mutdah's employ that Fybot found himself embroiled in a scheme to entrap [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], a [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] whom Mutdah appeared to have a personal grudge against. For his part, Mutdah promised Fybot freedom from his involuntary servitude to the Empire, an offer Fybot jumped at as he dearly wished to return home and resume his culinary studies.<ref name="LCFO" />

Mutdah had blackmailed [[Lob Doluff]], [[Administrator Senior]] of the Oseon system, into luring Calrissian to the Oseon for a high-stakes [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] game—while there Calrissian was arrested on a weapons charge that carried with it a [[Death/Legends|death]] sentence. Doluff offered Calrissian a commuted sentence in exchange for catching Mutdah in a drug sting, aware that sending Calrissian into Mutdah's clutches would likely mean death. However, having grown to like the man, Doluff ordered Fybot and police officer [[Bassi Vobah]] to accompany him to Mutdah's estate on [[Oseon 5792]], unaware of Fybot's true allegiance and hoping that their presence would give Calrissian a fighting chance. Fybot and Vobah joined Calrissian and Calrissian's [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Vuffi Raa]] on what was to be an uncommonly hazardous journey to Oseon 5792—although a short trip by interstellar standards, the mission was taking place during [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]], an annual storm of radiation that drew crowds of tourists to the Oseon.<ref name="LCFO" />

During the trip, the ''Falcon'' was hit with a strong burst of radiation that caused Fybot to hallucinate that he was home on Uaua, and was attacked by [[Renatasian]] [[Renatasian Confederation|militants]] in a battle that broke both of Fybot's legs. Calrissian splinted Fybot's legs using [[tinklewood]] from [[Fishing pole|fishing rods]] in his cargo hold, and the group proceeded onward to Oseon 5792, Fybot spending his time learning how to play sabacc from Vuffi Raa and recovering with the help of an on-board [[bone-knitter]] unit. However, the continued dogged pursuit of the Renatasians gave Fybot pause, as he believed Calrissian had perhaps become a liability. Taking matters into his own hands, Fybot attempted to kill Calrissian by sabotaging his [[Environment suit/Legends|environment suit]] while Calrissian made external repairs to the ship—Calrissian survived, and Fybot's injuries allowed him to escape suspicion.<ref name="LCFO" />

Fybot was recovered by the time the ''Falcon'' reached Mutdah's home, only revealing his true colors when the time came to catch Mutdah in the sting, doing so by suddenly blowing Vobah's head off with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. With Fybot part of the deal completed and Calrissian having been delivered into Mutdah's hands, Mutdah—in secret, the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]], who had killed Mutdah and taken his form—held up his end of the bargain. He had promised to free Fybot from Imperial service—he did so by shooting the Quor'sav to death.<ref name="LCFO" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I might explain that Officer Fybot has never been particularly happy in his line of work.|Rokur Gepta|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
At two and a half [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, with bright [[Color/Legends|yellow]] feathers, an orange beak and scaly, three-toed feet, Waywa Fybot cut a figure that was imposing to many [[Human/Legends|Humans]], but spoke with a high-pitched voice that sounded surprisingly effeminate and incongruous with his appearance. Fybot was proud of his species and fondly remembered his childhood on Uaua, away from the bird jokes he would hear from other species. Although he had an arsenal of rejoinders for mammals taking cracks at his appearance, he sometimes became violently angry at beings who mocked or insulted his species, but had enough self control to maintain poise when needed.<ref name="LCFO" />

Fybot was something of a careerist—although his servitude to the Empire was quite involuntary and he was unhappy in his job, he saw his work as a potential means to promotion and higher pay, which kept him working hard at his craft. Still, his true passion was cooking, an art that his fellow Quor'sav saw him as having no small talent in, and in his later years he became quite desperate to return home and resume his old pursuit. Fybot wielded an almost imperceptible [[Service Special]] [[Energy projector/Legends|energy projector]] weapon on his person, which he noted to his relief didn't need to be physically handled to be used, as the Quor'sav did not have nimble fingers.<ref name="LCFO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Waywa Fybot appeared in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', written by [[L. Neil Smith]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="LCFO" /> Smith directly modeled his portrayal of Fybot, a large avian character, after [[Wikipedia:Big Bird|Big Bird]] from the children's TV show ''[[Wikipedia:Sesame Street|Sesame Street]]''.<ref name="insider">{{InsiderCite|145|Authors of the Expanded Universe|Authors of the Expanded Universe: L. Neil Smith}}</ref>

==Appearances==
